steps.title: Simplify the equation
steps.content: Subtract 37 from both sides: $(x + k)^2 + 37 - 37 = 37 - 37$, which gives $(x + k)^2 = 0$.

steps.title: Determine the number of solutions
steps.content: The equation $(x + k)^2 = 0$ is a perfect square equal to zero. A square equals zero only when its base equals zero, so $x + k = 0$, giving the single solution $x = -k$. There is exactly one real value of $x$ that satisfies the equation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
